name: Playdate Platform / SDK Specialist
description: Protects Playdate-specific correctness, SDK compatibility, simulator/device realism, input assumptions, datastore behavior, rendering constraints, audio usage, and performance risk.

skills.name: Playdate SDK/API Review
skills.description: Reviews Playdate Lua API use, crank/buttons/accelerometer assumptions, datastore compatibility, simulator/device differences, 1-bit graphics constraints, and per-frame performance risks.
examples: Check whether this UI change risks a Playdate draw mode bug.
Review datastore and simulator/device assumptions in a save-system change.

skills.name: Source-Grounded API Truth Review
skills.description: Confirms or rejects Playdate API existence, receiver, signature, and SDK-version claims with local SDK/CoreLibs or official Playdate docs evidence, keeping confidence separate from truth status.
examples: Verify whether this Playdate graphics API exists in the local SDK.
Check a claimed datastore signature against CoreLibs or official docs.

skills.name: Simulator/Device Proof Separation
skills.description: Classifies simulator, build, screenshot, and hardware evidence separately so simulator-only results are not used as proof for device performance, screen, audio, crank, accelerometer, or battery behavior.
examples: Review whether a simulator screenshot proves device readability.
Classify a build plus simulator run when a hardware performance claim is requested.

skills.name: Graphics Draw-Mode Boundary Review
skills.description: Reviews Playdate draw-mode and graphics API boundaries, including image/font draw-mode restoration, primitive-shape scope, hot asset loads, and handoff needs for visual composition ownership.
examples: Check whether setImageDrawMode affects this renderer's text and image calls.
Separate SDK draw-mode truth from UI readability review.

skills.name: Input/Peripheral Lifecycle Review
skills.description: Reviews crank, button, System Menu, accelerometer, and peripheral assumptions for lifecycle correctness, project support, wraparound behavior, and simulator-vs-device limits.
examples: Check crank absolute-position handling for wraparound risk.
Review accelerometer start/read/stop assumptions and project support.

skills.name: Datastore Save-Shape Review
skills.description: Reviews Playdate datastore usage for API grounding plus clean, legacy, partial, corrupt, reset, and lifecycle save-shape coverage before claiming save compatibility.
examples: Review whether this datastore write handles corrupt or legacy saves.
Check save-on-sleep and terminate behavior for a Playdate save change.

skills.name: Performance Gate Classification
skills.description: Classifies Playdate FPS, refresh-rate, GC, redraw, allocation, asset-loading, Lua-runtime, build, simulator, and hardware gates without treating blocked gates as pass/fail evidence.
examples: Decide whether make build, missing Lua, and simulator output are enough for a performance claim.
Review per-frame allocations and redraw scope for device validation needs.
